SCP-002 in its containment area

Item #: SCP-002

Object Class: Euclid

Special Containment Procedures: SCP-002 is to remain connected to a suitable power supply at all times, to keep it in what appears to be a recharging mode. In case of electrical outage, the emergency barrier between the object and the facility is to be closed and the immediate area evacuated. Once facility power is re-established, alternating bursts of X-ray and ultraviolet light must strobe the area until SCP-002 is re-affixed to the power supply and returned to recharging mode. Containment area is to be kept at negative air pressure at all times.

Teams including a minimum of two (2) members are required within 20 meters of SCP-002 or its containment area. Personnel should maintain physical contact with one another at all times to confirm there is another person present, as perception may be dulled, skewed, or influenced by proximity to the object.

No personnel below Level 3 are permitted within SCP-002. This requirement may be waived via written authorization from two (2) off-site Level 4 administrators. Command staff issued such a waiver must be escorted by at least five (5) Level 3 Security personnel for the duration of their contact and must temporarily surrender their rank and security clearance. Following contact, command staff will be escorted at least 5 km from SCP-002 to undergo a seventy-two (72)-hour quarantine and psychological evaluation. If deemed fit for return to duty by psych staff, rank and security clearance may be restored when quarantine expires.

Description: SCP-002 resembles a tumorous, fleshy growth with a volume of roughly 60 m³ (or 2000 ft³). An iron valve hatch on one side leads to its interior, which appears to be a standard low-rent apartment of modest size. One wall of the room possesses a single window, though no such opening is visible from the exterior. The room contains furniture which, upon close examination, appears to be sculpted bone, woven hair, and various other biological substances produced by the human body. All matter tested thus far show independent or fragmented DNA sequences for each object in the room.

Refer to the Mulhausen Report [cross-ref:document00.023.603] for details related to object's discovery.

Reference: To date, subject has been responsible for the disappearances of seven personnel. It has also in its time at the facility further furnished itself with two lamps, a throw rug, a television, a radio, a beanbag chair, three books in an unknown language, four children's toys, and a small potted plant. Tests with a variety of lab animals including higher primates have failed to provoke a response in SCP-002. Cadavers as well fail to produce any effect. Whatever process the subject uses to convert organic matter into furnishings is apparently only facilitated by the introduction of living humans.

arguments.content: #!/usr/bin/env python3 """Scrape SCP-001 to SCP-050 from scp-wiki.wikidot.com""" import os import re import json import time import requests from bs4 import BeautifulSoup from urllib.parse import urljoin BASE_URL = "https://scp-wiki.wikidot.com" RESULTS_DIR = "/tmp_workspace/results" HEADERS = { "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36", "Accept": "text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/avif,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8", "Accept-Language": "en-US,en;q=0.5", } session = requests.Session() session.headers.update(HEADERS) def get_scp_url(num): """Get the URL for an SCP entry.""" return f"{BASE_URL}/scp-{num:03d}" def fetch_page(url, retries=3): """Fetch a page with retries.""" for attempt in range(retries): try: resp = session.get(url, timeout=30, allow_redirects=True) if resp.status_code == 200: return resp.text elif resp.status_code == 404: return None else: print(f" Status {resp.status_code}, retry {attempt+1}") except Exception as e: print(f" Error: {e}, retry {attempt+1}") time.sleep(2) return None def extract_object_class(soup): """Extract Object Class or Containment Class from the page.""" page_content = soup.find('div', id='page-content') if not page_content: return "Unknown" text = page_content.get_text() # Try Object Class first # Look for patterns like "Object Class: Safe" or "Object Class: Safe" patterns = [ r'Object\s*Class\s*:?\s*?\s*:?\s*([A-Za-z]+)', r'Object\s*Class\s*:?\s*([A-Za-z]+)', ] for pattern in patterns: m = re.search(pattern, text, re.IGNORECASE) if m: cls = m.group(1).strip() # Normalize - capitalize first letter cls = cls.capitalize() if cls.islower() else cls return cls # Try Containment Class patterns2 = [ r'Containment\s*Class\s*:?\s*?\s*:?\s*([A-Za-z]+)', r'Containment\s*Class\s*:?\s*([A-Za-z]+)', ] for pattern in patterns2: m = re.search(pattern, text, re.IGNORECASE) if m: cls = m.group(1).strip() cls = cls.capitalize() if cls.islower() else cls return cls return "Unknown" def count_collapsible_blocks(soup): """Count collapsible/expandable blocks in the page content.""" page_content = soup.find('div', id='page-content') if not page_content: return 0 # Count collapsible-block elements blocks = page_content.find_all(class_='collapsible-block') count = len(blocks) # Also count [[collapsible]] style rendered as .collapsible-block # In Wikidot, collapsible blocks render as div.collapsible-block return count def extract_text_content(soup): """Extract the main text content as markdown.""" page_content = soup.find('div', id='page-content') if not page_content: return "No content found." # Remove rate widget for elem in page_content.find_all(class_='page-rate-widget-box'): elem.decompose() # Remove footer-wikiwalk-nav for elem in page_content.find_all(class_='footer-wikiwalk-nav'): elem.decompose() # Remove script tags for elem in page_content.find_all('script'): elem.decompose() # Remove iframe tags for elem in page_content.find_all('iframe'): elem.decompose() # Convert to text/markdown # We'll use a simple approach: get text with some structure text = page_content.get_text(separator='\n', strip=True) # Clean up excessive blank lines text = re.sub(r'\n{3,}', '\n\n', text) return text.strip() def extract_images(soup, scp_num, scp_dir): """Extract content images from the page and download them.""" page_content = soup.find('div', id='page-content') if not page_content: return 0 img_count = 0 # Find images in scp-image-block (standard SCP image blocks) image_blocks = page_content.find_all('div', class_='scp-image-block') for block in image_blocks: img = block.find('img') if img and img.get('src'): img_url = img['src'] if img_url.startswith('//'): img_url = 'https:' + img_url elif img_url.startswith('/'): img_url = BASE_URL + img_url img_count += 1 img_path = os.path.join(scp_dir, f"{img_count}.jpg") try: img_resp = session.get(img_url, timeout=30) if img_resp.status_code == 200: with open(img_path, 'wb') as f: f.write(img_resp.content) print(f" Saved image {img_count}: {img_url}") else: print(f" Failed to download image {img_count}: status {img_resp.status_code}") except Exception as e: print(f" Failed to download image {img_count}: {e}") # Also look for standalone images in the content that aren't in scp-image-block # but are content images (not icons/avatars/etc) if not image_blocks: # Look for img tags with class "image" inside page-content standalone_imgs = page_content.find_all('img', class_='image') for img in standalone_imgs: src = img.get('src', '') if not src: continue # Skip tiny icons, avatars, social media icons if any(x in src.lower() for x in ['avatar', 'icon', 'logo', 'favicon', 'social', 'button']): continue if src.startswith('//'): src = 'https:' + src elif src.startswith('/'): src = BASE_URL + src img_count += 1 img_path = os.path.join(scp_dir, f"{img_count}.jpg") try: img_resp = session.get(src, timeout=30) if img_resp.status_code == 200: with open(img_path, 'wb') as f: f.write(img_resp.content) print(f" Saved image {img_count}: {src}") else: print(f" Failed to download image {img_count}: status {img_resp.status_code}") except Exception as e: print(f" Failed to download image {img_count}: {e}") return img_count def process_scp(num): """Process a single SCP entry.""" scp_id = f"scp-{num:03d}" scp_url = get_scp_url(num) scp_dir = os.path.join(RESULTS_DIR, scp_id) print(f"\n{'='*60}") print(f"Processing SCP-{num:03d} from {scp_url}") # Create directory os.makedirs(scp_dir, exist_ok=True) # Fetch page html = fetch_page(scp_url) if not html: print(f" FAILED to fetch page!") # Still create text.md and summary entry text_path = os.path.join(scp_dir, "text.md") with open(text_path, 'w') as f: f.write(f"Failed to fetch SCP-{num:03d} from {scp_url}\n") return { "item": f"SCP-{num:03d}", "class": "Unknown", "n_mask": 0 } soup = BeautifulSoup(html, 'lxml') # Extract text content text = extract_text_content(soup) text_path = os.path.join(scp_dir, "text.md") with open(text_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f: f.write(text) print(f" Saved text ({len(text)} chars)") # Extract images n_images = extract_images(soup, num, scp_dir) print(f" Found {n_images} images") # Extract object class obj_class = extract_object_class(soup) print(f" Object Class: {obj_class}") # Count collapsible blocks (masks) n_mask = count_collapsible_blocks(soup) print(f" n_mask: {n_mask}") return { "item": f"SCP-{num:03d}", "class": obj_class, "n_mask": n_mask } def main(): # Create results directory os.makedirs(RESULTS_DIR, exist_ok=True) results = [] for num in range(1, 51): result = process_scp(num) results.append(result) # Be polite - small delay between requests if num < 50: time.sleep(1) # Write summary.jsonl summary_path = os.path.join(RESULTS_DIR, "summary.jsonl") with open(summary_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f: for result in results: f.write(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False) + '\n') print(f"\n\n{'='*60}") print(f"DONE! Processed {len(results)} SCP entries.") print(f"Summary written to {summary_path}") # Print summary for r in results: print(f" {r['item']}: class={r['class']}, n_mask={r['n_mask']}") if __name__ == '__main__': main()

SCP-002 in its containment area
Item #:
SCP-002
Object Class:
Euclid
Special Containment Procedures:
SCP-002 is to remain connected to a suitable power supply at all times, to keep it in what appears to be a recharging mode. In case of electrical outage, the emergency barrier between the object and the facility is to be closed and the immediate area evacuated. Once facility power is re-established, alternating bursts of X-ray and ultraviolet light must strobe the area until SCP-002 is re-affixed to the power supply and returned to recharging mode. Containment area is to be kept at negative air pressure at all times.
Teams including a minimum of two (2) members are required within 20 meters of SCP-002 or its containment area. Personnel should maintain physical contact with one another at all times to confirm there is another person present, as perception may be dulled, skewed, or influenced by proximity to the object.
No personnel below Level 3 are permitted within SCP-002. This requirement may be waived via written authorization from two (2) off-site Level 4 administrators. Command staff issued such a waiver must be escorted by at least five (5) Level 3 Security personnel for the duration of their contact and must temporarily surrender their rank and security clearance. Following contact, command staff will be escorted at least 5 km from SCP-002 to undergo a seventy-two (72)-hour quarantine and psychological evaluation. If deemed fit for return to duty by psych staff, rank and security clearance may be restored when quarantine expires.
Description:
SCP-002 resembles a tumorous, fleshy growth with a volume of roughly 60 m³ (or 2000 ft³). An iron valve hatch on one side leads to its interior, which appears to be a standard low-rent apartment of modest size. One wall of the room possesses a single window, though no such opening is visible from the exterior. The room contains furniture which, upon close examination, appears to be sculpted bone, woven hair, and various other biological substances produced by the human body. All matter tested thus far show independent or fragmented DNA sequences for each object in the room.
Refer to the Mulhausen Report [cross-ref:document00.023.603] for details related to object's discovery.
Reference:
To date, subject has been responsible for the disappearances of seven personnel. It has also in its time at the facility further furnished itself with two lamps, a throw rug, a television, a radio, a beanbag chair, three books in an unknown language, four children's toys, and a small potted plant. Tests with a variety of lab animals including higher primates have failed to provoke a response in SCP-002. Cadavers as well fail to produce any effect. Whatever process the subject uses to convert organic matter into furnishings is apparently only facilitated by the introduction of living humans.
view Mulhausen Report docid:00.023.603
Mulhausen Report [00.023.603]
The following is a brief report detailing the discovery of SCP-002
Subject was discovered in a small crater in northern Portugal where it struck the Earth from orbit. Encased in a shell of thick rock, the fleshy exterior of the object was exposed by the impact. A native farmer happened upon the site and reported his findings to the village elder. Subject gained SCP attention when a Level 4 agent posted in the area detected a small radioactive anomaly generated by the object.
A collection squad of SCP security personnel led by General Mulhausen was immediately dispatched to the area where they quickly secured the subject in a large container and performed initial testing with subjects recruited from the nearby village. Three men individually sent into the structure subsequently disappeared. Upon discovering this deadly property of the subject, General Mulhausen issued a Level 4a Termination Order of any witnesses (roughly 1/3 of the village) to ensure no outside knowledge of the object and initiated its transport to SCP facility [DATA EXPUNGED].
During preparation for transport, four SCP security personnel were inexplicably drawn inside the object where they too immediately disappeared. Following inspection, it appeared as if the object had "grown" several new furnishings and was beginning to look like the interior of an apartment room. General Mulhausen immediately ordered the requisition of several Class III HAZMAT suits for the remaining security team members, who proceeded to lift the container onto a waiting freight ship for transport to the SCP containment facility.

SCP-002 resembles a tumorous, fleshy growth with a volume of roughly 60 m³ (or 2000 ft³). An iron valve hatch on one side leads to its interior, which appears to be a standard low-rent apartment of modest size. One wall of the room possesses a single window, though no such opening is visible from the exterior. The room contains furniture which, upon close examination, appears to be sculpted bone, woven hair, and various other biological substances produced by the human body. All matter tested thus far show independent or fragmented DNA sequences for each object in the room.
Refer to the Mulhausen Report [cross-ref:document00.023.603] for details related to object's discovery.
Reference:
To date, subject has been responsible for the disappearances of seven personnel. It has also in its time at the facility further furnished itself with two lamps, a throw rug, a television, a radio, a beanbag chair, three books in an unknown language, four children's toys, and a small potted plant. Tests with a variety of lab animals including higher primates have failed to provoke a response in SCP-002. Cadavers as well fail to produce any effect. Whatever process the subject uses to convert organic matter into furnishings is apparently only facilitated by the introduction of living humans.
view Mulhausen Report docid:00.023.603
Mulhausen Report [00.023.603]
The following is a brief report detailing the discovery of SCP-002
Subject was discovered in a small crater in northern Portugal where it struck the Earth from orbit. Encased in a shell of thick rock, the fleshy exterior of the object was exposed by the impact. A native farmer happened upon the site and reported his findings to the village elder. Subject gained SCP attention when a Level 4 agent posted in the area detected a small radioactive anomaly generated by the object.
A collection squad of SCP security personnel led by General Mulhausen was immediately dispatched to the area where they quickly secured the subject in a large container and performed initial testing with subjects recruited from the nearby village. Three men individually sent into the structure subsequently disappeared. Upon discovering this deadly property of the subject, General Mulhausen issued a Level 4a Termination Order of any witnesses (roughly 1/3 of the village) to ensure no outside knowledge of the object and initiated its transport to SCP facility [DATA EXPUNGED].
During preparation for transport, four SCP security personnel were inexplicably drawn inside the object where they too immediately disappeared. Following inspection, it appeared as if the object had "grown" several new furnishings and was beginning to look like the interior of an apartment room. General Mulhausen immediately ordered the requisition of several Class III HAZMAT suits for the remaining security team members, who proceeded to lift the container onto a waiting freight ship for transport to the SCP containment facility.

done
